microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's enterprise resilience program from a 2-person operation into a **platformized system**, synthesizing partner needs from Databricks, Walmart, SAP, and NetApp into reusable primitives that sustained 45+ annual drills and drove **$14M+ business impact**.

Led 0→1 development of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining product requirements and self-service scheduling capabilities that translated one-off partner requests into **durable, reusable features** and reduced operational toil by 30%.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive resilience testing capability by identifying a critical platform gap through enterprise partner feedback, delivering a rack-level drill program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate** and establishing a new model for continuous validation.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract** — owning GTM readiness, stakeholder alignment, and post-execution reporting end-to-end.

Scaled team leverage through AI-driven automation by building an internal AI agent and restructuring planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by **39%** and increasing drill capacity by 21% without adding headcount.

Partnered with engineering and design counterparts to build a short-, medium-, and long-term roadmap for the resilience platform, creating clarity and accountability at every horizon and ensuring enterprise-facing work laddered up into **scalable platform capabilities**.
microsoft_2023: Drove **product adoption** for AI-driven code generation workflows across Azure service teams, conducting demos and training that boosted utilization across 14 key teams and saved **37 engineering hours monthly** — directly mirroring a creator-empathy, platform-rollout motion.

Shaped the product roadmap to include intent-based YAML generation by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and packaging synthesized insights into clear, prioritized product requirements for the engineering team.

Improved data integrity and creator-side discoverability by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool, cutting lookup time by 83% and enabling self-serve access across the organization.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework — translating bespoke team requests from 140+ service teams into a single, reusable execution roadmap.

Built a platform product strategy to eliminate operational toil during region launches, conducting discovery with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of manual effort** per region and building a phased roadmap to automate critical paths into durable, scalable systems.

Enabled leadership to prioritize high-impact automation gaps by engineering a **Power BI dashboard** tracking operational toil across 140+ teams, turning raw partner data into executive-ready insights that directly informed roadmap sequencing and resource allocation.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by owning the legacy OS migration strategy end-to-end — mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations and translating complex partner constraints into a sequenced, reusable deployment framework.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets**, stabilize the developer intake process, and create accountability for a cross-functional team of engineers and operations stakeholders.

Drove strategic alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement automated CI/CD pipelines, accelerating the software deployment cycle by **25%** and establishing a scalable, self-service release model that reduced per-team coordination overhead.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by owning the end-to-end go-to-market motion for 200+ monthly proposals — redesigning the CRM workflow to standardize the scoping and invoicing lifecycle and reduce friction between sales and operations.

Reduced Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and boosted conversion rate by 2.7% by executing a digital-first GTM strategy: optimizing the website experience, refining the Google Business profile, and building a repeatable acquisition funnel grounded in audience data.

Improved leadership pricing decisions by conducting a **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying margin leaks and delivering a data-backed framework that informed subscription and packaging strategy going forward.

tailoring_notes: 'Reusable primitives' and 'durable, reusable features' (JD: 'reusable primitives, systems, and platform capabilities') woven into microsoft_ft bullets 1 and 2.
'Synthesizing partner needs' and 'translating one-off partner requests' (JD: 'synthesize their requests', 'translate one-off requests') used in microsoft_ft bullet 1 and microsoft_2022 bullet 1.
'GTM readiness' and 'go-to-market motion' (JD: 'own the product marketing and go-to-market strategy') surfaced in microsoft_ft bullet 4 and pro_painters bullet 1.
'Creator empathy' and 'platform rollout motion' (JD: 'creator empathy, platform thinking') referenced in microsoft_2023 bullet 1 to reframe an Azure adoption story in Substack-relevant vocabulary.
'Executive alignment' and 'laddered up into scalable platform capabilities' (JD: 'lock-step with company strategy', 'ladders up into reusable primitives') used in microsoft_ft bullets 4 and 6.
